sections.title: 📄 Paper of the day — LLM-as-judge overfits to its own feedback

item.title: Exploiting LLM-as-a-Judge Disposition on Free Text Legal QA via Prompt Optimization
item.authors: Mohamed Hesham Elganayni, Runsheng Chen, Sebastian Nagl, Matthias Grabmair
item.tldr: Prompts auto-optimized against a LENIENT LLM-judge transfer well to strict judges; the reverse does not. Strict-judge feedback overfits prompts to that specific judge.
item.why: Directly relevant to MAE's 10 LLM-judge evaluators — picking judge disposition during prompt/agent optimization changes how well the system generalizes. 'Optimize against lenient, validate against strict' is a cheap calibration recipe.
key_findings: ProTeGi auto-optimization consistently beats human-written task prompts across 4 task models.
Lenient judge → higher, more consistent gains; prompts transfer across judges.
Strict judge → restrictive feedback; prompts overfit and fail cross-judge.
Implication for eval-driven-dev: judge choice during optimization IS a hyperparameter.

sections.title: 📰 Industry pulse — 3 from HN front page

items.title: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports
items.summary: Postmortem on Claude Code quality regressions users flagged this month — covers routing / infra issues and what changed.
items.why: You ship with Claude Code (Hermes) daily. Worth a 5-min read to know which past frustrations were real infra and which were hallucinated.

items.title: DeepSeek v4
items.summary: DeepSeek v4 API docs drop on HN #2 front-page. Pricing / context / tool-use details are what matter for agent stacks.
items.why: DeepSeek-V3 already features in the paper above as one of the LLM-judges. V4 could be a cheaper judge-layer candidate for MAE's 10 L3 evaluators.

items.title: Hear your agent suffer through your code (endless-toil)
items.summary: Tiny tool that turns your agent's tool-call log into audio so you can literally hear it struggle. Cute and also a real observability idea.
items.why: A lo-fi observability pattern — great mental model for the Hermes/MAE debugging loop. Maybe a weekend 1-hour port.

items.title: Lenient-judge optimization pass for MAE L3
items.quote: Optimize prompts using a lenient L3 judge, then validate against the strict one. Per today's paper, this transfers; reverse doesn't.

items.title: Endless-toil-style audio log for Hermes agent runs
items.quote: Pipe tool-call events into short audio cues. 'Hear' the overnight run instead of scrolling logs in the morning.

items.title: Judge-disposition knob in daily brief
items.quote: Add a `judge_mode: lenient|strict` field to the brief JSON — agent picks itself vs. user. Tiny UX, big signal for prompt research.
